WATERGATE TENANTS ASSOCIATION
9 COMMODORE DRIVE, #304
Emeryville, California 94608

July 24, 1978


Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 "K" Street
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Attn: Mr. Daniel H. Lowenstein, Chairman

RE: Conflict of Interest Laws Applicable to
Emeryville City Council Members

Dear Sirs:

This letter is a confirmation of our July 23rd Mailgram to you about the
above matter. In this regard, we enclose copies of the following:

1. Our July 23rd letter sent to you as a Mailgram.

2. The June 15th letter from Pacific Union Company, representing their
initial communication to the tenants and proposing a month-to-month
tenancy when leases expire.

3. Our June 29th letter to Pacific Union Company.

4. Their July 13th letter to tenants outlining some tentative prices, etc.

5. Our July 21st letter to the Chairman of the Planning Commission calling
for consideration of our proposed ordinance before final approval of
the proposal is granted.

We would like your timely consideration of the questions posed in our July 23rd
letter.

Sincerely yours,

Michael Levin
President

ML:rms
Encls.
cc: Mr. Chris Burns
Acting Vice-President
Watergate Tenants Association

Emeryville City Council Members (w/enclosures)
Wallace Fox - Mayor
Wylie Eaton - Vice-Mayor
Richard Kassis
Rena Rickles
Esther Spriggs

Committees: Security □ Maintenance □ Education and Bylaws □ Recreational and Social Activities

WATERGATE TENANTS ASSOCIATION
9 COMMODORE DRIVE, #304
Emeryville, California 94608

July 23, 1978


Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 "K" Street
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Attn: Mr. Daniel H. Lowenstein, Chairman

RE: Conflict Of Interest Laws Applicable To
Emeryville City Council Members

Dear Sirs:

Thank you for sending us a copy of the July 14th letter to you from
James White, City Attorney for Emeryville. The purpose of this letter is to
present additional background information to you which we believe to be relevant
in considering Mr. White's request, and to present to you our point of view.

It should be understood that this proposed conversion is not a "routine"
conversion. In size, it is the largest conversion ever attempted in California
and, as far as we know, it is the largest such attempt ever in the United States.
Watergate represents a unique situation in America. With 2000 residents, we
represent more than 50% of the adult population of Emeryville. Of 2,608 registered
voters in Emeryville, 53% or 1,267 live at Watergate. The 2,000 residents have
opted for a life in an alternative complex having no children and, if the con-
version is completed, there will be no comparable housing available nearby for
those who cannot afford what appears to be very high prices for units constructed
subject to minimum building codes on filled land.

So much for background. With respect to the conflict of interest described
in Mr. White's letter, it is based on the fact that the three City Council Members
who live at Watergate will be "permitted to purchase their unit for approximately
$4,000 less than the purchase price offered to the general public." In actuality,
the discount will be 6% of the purchase price; a copy of the July 13th letter to
residents from Pacific Union Company will be mailed under separate cover de-
scribing this situation. We will also send you, as information, a copy of the
June 15th letter from Pacific Union representing the first communication to
tenants from the new owners.

We do not believe that a conflict of interest exists for the three Council
Members who live at Watergate simply because they are not, to our knowledge, being
offered any different financial discount than is being offered to the other 2,000
residents at Watergate. We believe that the potential conflict can be easily
solved by having Council Members involved disclose to the City the estimated
financial impact involved in their individual situations. The problem is further


Committees: Security □ Maintenance □ Education and Bylaws □ Recreational and Social Activities

Fair Political Practices Commission
July 23, 1978
Page 2

complicated by the fact that these three constitute a majority of the
Council Members and, if they are not allowed to vote on this measure,
that leaves only two other Council Members.

The initial question is whether or not an ordinance requiring
approval of 50% plus 1 of the existing tenants before approval of the
City Council and the Planning Commission should be enacted. We believe
that the members of City Council should be allowed to vote on this matter
affecting more than 50% of the adults in this City.

We would appreciate a timely reply from the Commission about this
matter. If you have any questions, please call me at (415) 445-5809, or
Chris Burns, Acting Vice-President at (415) 653-7378.

Sincerely yours,

Michael Levin
President
Watergate Tenants Association

ML:rms

June 15, 1978


Dear Watergate Resident:

On June 1, 1978 we became the new owners of the Watergate Apartments.
Our group is composed of the Pacific Union Company of San Francisco
and The Watergate Company. We are proud of the Watergate apartment
complex and look forward to the challenge of maintaining and improving
the pleasant quality of life in this community.

Our first objective is to manage Watergate as the best apartment complex
possible.

We plan to go slowly and make as few changes as possible until we are
familiar with all aspects of the complex's operation and thus our current
rental policy for existing residents will remain the same. All leases
will be respected, of course, as a lease is a valid contract regardless
of who owns the complex. Residents whose leases expire will not be
required to re-lease and will automatically go on a month to month tenancy
with no increase in rent. The policy of adding electric metering to units
whose leases expire will continue as before.

We have received many inquiries regarding the possible conversion of
Watergate to condominiums. We have no specific information that will be
helpful to you at this time. However, we will be contacting each resident
in the near future to discuss the possibilities and answer your questions
as best we can.

Should you have any questions, please come to the office and we will be
glad to help in any way we can. We look forward to a long and pleasant
association with you and with Watergate.

Sincerely,

PACIFIC UNION COMPANY                THE WATERGATE COMPANY

W. F. Tunney                         Laurie E. McBride

W.F. Tunney                          Laurie E. McBride


WATERGATE                    TEN COMMODORE DRIVE/EMERYVILLE, CALIFORNIA 94608/(415) 654-7666

COPY

WATERGATE TENANTS ASSOCIATION
Admiral Drive #231
Emeryville, California 94608

9 Commodore Drive #304
June 29, 1978

Laurie E. McBride                W. F. Tunney
The Watergate Company and Pacific Union Company
10 Commodore Drive, Emeryville, California 94608

Many tenants who found your letter of June 15, 1978
under their doors have expressed their concern because of a lack
of clarity as to the intent of the new owners, viz:

1. Tenants question your failure to offer renewal of their
   leases as heretofore, without an adequate explanation.

2. A policy of renting to tenants on a month-to-month basis
   removes the feeling of stability at the Watergate complex
   that was present when leases were offered for periods
   of 12 to 18 months.

3. Since your letter states that you "have no specific
   information that will be helpful....at this time"
   with regard to a possible conversion to condominiums,
   tenants see no reason for being individually contacted
   now.

4. Before further contact is made, tenants are entitled to
   a clear statement of your intent so that they can plan
   accordingly.

Therefore, the Watergate Tenant's Association respectfully
requests that no further contact of tenants be made with regard to
the matter of condominiums until you can provide specific sales
proposals.

Sincerely,

WATERGATE TENANTS ASSOCIATION

By: [signature]
Michael Levin President

C.C. To Watergate Tenants

Watergate Residents please mark your calendar:

July 23rd 7:00 P.M. General Meeting at the Clipper Club which will
                    take up matters of interest to you.

And a reminder for Sunday, July 2nd from 12:00 to 5:00 P.M.
Pre Holiday Pow Wow - Poolside by the Clipper Club

Committees: Security □ Maintenance □ Education and Bylaws □ Recreational and Social Activities

July 13, 1978

PACIFIC UNION Co.
3701 Buchanan Street
San Francisco
California 94123

Phone (415) 929-7100


Dear Watergate Resident:

We would like to give you specific information regarding our conversion plans
here at Watergate.

It is our hope that this complex will continue to provide you with a unique home
lifestyle, close to the geographical center of the San Francisco Bay Area. Our
plan is to provide each of you the increased advantage of having the opportunity
to own the unit in which you reside. This will be done by converting the apartment
complex from one ownership to condominiums, where 1,249 individuals own their own
unit and share the ownership of the common areas such as land, streets, Clipper
Club, etc., through a homeowner's association.

While we are conducting this process, the Company has agreed for the present not
to increase the rents to current residents and to offer six month renewals on
expired leases.

We plan to keep the present operational staff and only make administrative changes
where it is deemed an improvement.

There are tremendous advantages to you in the conversion process and we hope those
of you who plan on staying here recognize the benefits. In addition to being in
a position to purchase your home at a favorable price, several improvements will
occur here at Watergate.

Watergate Company intends as part of its plans, to correct the deficiencies that
have been identified in the project, with special emphasis on security, water
leaks and common area rehabilitation. It has been shown that people will have
a greater commitment because of their decision to own here and the population will
become less transient. The City of Emeryville will share in the benefits of a
stable population.

We are aware that there are those of you who are not in a financial position to
consider buying or who will elect not to buy but would like to stay on as renters.
In this regard, you should understand that we expect the phasing program to be

[illegible]

Watergate Residents                                                  Page Two
                                                                               July 13, 1978

such that the units will be sold over a three to four year period. During this
time, the unsold units will continue to be available as rentals. In addition,
even after all the units have been sold, there will be a significant number of
units available as rentals since some buyers will choose not to live in the pro-
ject.

In order for us to get this project into a position that it can be sold to you,
a number of things must occur. The entire project must be engineered for legal
subdivision spaces. The City Planning Department must approve the process and the
City Council must confirm the Planning Department's approval. Also, protective
covenants and restrictions and by-laws must be approved by the California Division
of Real Estate. Lenders must commit to provide loans for the units and most im-
portantly, "the marketplace" must approve in order for sales to be made.

You are in the attractive position of having all of this take place before you
make a decision about your purchase. We would like to present several of the
positive reasons why you should consider purchasing your unit.

PRICE:     Although we cannot quote an exact price for your unit until we have
           received all of the necessary approvals, we have received permission
           from the Division of Real Estate to quote price ranges. Please note
           that these proposed market price ranges are effective as of July,
           1978, i.e., as if the project could be sold today.

                                                                Average Price
           Type                Square Footage        Prices From    Less Than

           Studio              470 - 530 sq. ft.     $29,900        $35,000
           Jr. 1 Bedroom       530 - 716 sq. ft.     $35,000        $39,900
           1 Bedroom           668 - 944 sq. ft.     $43,000        $55,000
           2 Bedroom           890 -1250 sq. ft.     $65,000        $80,000

           The fact that there are 92 different floor plans, all with different
           views and various interior conditions, must be considered in deter-
           mining the final price. The prices for units with extra desirability
           because of view or location will be higher than the price averages
           quoted above.

           These price ranges are significantly under market value. While our
           experience indicates that these prices will increase to the general
           public, the units will be offered to you within these price ranges to Watergate residents. Residents will receive a 6% discount from the actual
           prices established in the range indicated. A decorator's allowance
           will be offered for each unit as follows: Studio, $1,000; Junior
           One-Bedroom, $1,250; One-Bedroom, $1,500; and Two-Bedroom, $2,500.

Watergate Residents                                                                  Page Three
                                                                                                July 13, 1978

The decorator's discount will be offered in the form of an additional
cash discount or in the form of actual interior work done for you by
the Watergate Company.

OPTION:    Watergate residents will be offered a 60-day option to buy at the
           time the project becomes available for sale.

TERMS:     We cannot be specific on the exact terms at this time, however, we
           expect to be able to deliver the best available financing for prime
           residences. We are currently working with a major lender to arrange
           90% market rate loans for each unit.

HOME-      When the units have all been sold, a homeowner's association will be
OWNER'S    established. Our current analysis indicates that the monthly fee will
ASSOCIA-   average less than $75.00 per month (depending on the size of the unit)
TION:      and will include common area maintenance, insurance, operation of the
           Clipper Club, security, etc.

TAXES:     Exact property taxes will not be established until the effects of
           Proposition 13 are known. However, any benefits resulting from
           Proposition 13 will accrue to you directly as a home owner.

The following is an analysis of the effect of purchasing a typical unit costing
$50,000, assuming a $10,000 down payment and an 80%, 30 year loan of $40,000 at
10% interest.

                                          Cash Expense
                                          
                                          Monthly Mortgage Payment             $351.03
                                          Estimated Homeowner's Dues              75.00
                                          Estimated Property Taxes              83.33
                                          
                                          Total Monthly Expenses before tax    $ 509.36

                                   Computation of Tax Savings
                                   
                                          Monthly Interest on Loan            $333.33
                                          Estimated Taxes                      83.33
                                          
                                          Total Monthly Tax Deduction          $416.66
                                          Assume 40% Tax Bracket               x .40
                                          
                                          Cash Savings After Taxes            $166.53    (166.53)
                                          
                                          Actual Monthly Housing Cost for Owner            $ 342.83
                                          
                                          Market Rent for Same Unit                       $ 415.00

                                   NOTE: No consideration of possible apprecia-
                                         tion or original discount from market
                                         value.

Watergate Residents                                                    Page Four
                                                                                 July 13, 1978


If you have any requests for additional information about the conversion process,
please contact either Sales Manager, Gary Hacker or Project Manager, Bill Tunney.

The opportunity to own your own home will be offered to you at one of the most
exceptional values ever offered in the Bay Area residential market. We expect
to be able to make this offer to you officially by the first of next year. We
hope that you will be able to take advantage of this opportunity.

Sincerely,

PACIFIC UNION COMPANY

[signature]
Peter C. Stocker

PCS:nb

WATERGATE TENANTS ASSOCIATION
9 COMMODORE DRIVE, #304
Emeryville, California 94608

July 21, 1978


Mr. Gary Kalian
Chairman
Emeryville City Planning Commission
2449 Powell Street
Emeryville, California 94608

Dear Mr. Kalian:

     On Tuesday, July 25, 1978, the Emeryville City Council will consider a proposed
ordinance to the effect that, before approval is given to converting any apartment
complex having fifty (50) units or more to condominiums, approval should be obtained
from 50% plus 1 of the tenants who were in occupancy in the complex at the time of
filing the Tentative Map. In the case of the Watergate Apartment Complex, this
apparently occurred on July 11, 1978.

     The Watergate Tenants Association, representing approximately 50% of the adult
population of Emeryville, by this letter, informs the Planning Commission that the
Association would consider it totally inappropriate in the circumstances for the
Planning Commission to approve a conversion plan for the Watergate Apartment Complex
before the above mentioned ordinance has been considered and voted upon by Emeryville
City Council.

     We would like to hear your early response to this viewpoint.

                                                    Sincerely yours,


                                                    Michael Levin
                                                    President
                                                    Watergate Tenants Association

cc: Emeryville Planning Commission:          Emeryville City Council Members:
          Sally Benedetti                               Wallace Fox, Mayor
          Thomas Enos                                   Kylie Eaton, Vice-Mayor
          Anya Horvath                                  Richard Kassis
          Doyle Hudson                                  Rena Rickles
          Patricia Jones                                Esther Springs
          Carole Yocum

     James White, Emeryville City Attorney

     Roberta Horton, Emeryville City Clerk




Committees: Security □ Maintenance □ Education and Bylaws □ Recreational and Social Activities

WATERGATE TENANTS ASSOCIATION
6-A ADMIRAL DRIVE, #481
Emeryville, California 94608


August 17, 1978


Mr. James White
Emeryville City Attorney
Emeryville City Hall
2449 Powell Street
Emeryville, California  94608

Dear Jim:

At last night's "work session" of Emeryville City Planning Commission, you indicated that you would be receiving communications from other governmental agencies about the "conflict of interest" question concerning Gary Kalian and Ms. Patricia Jones in the August 23rd public hearing about the proposed Watergate condominium conversion.  We would like to receive copies of whatever transpires on this matter on a timely basis.

As you probably know, our position is that Gary, being an employee of F. P. Lathrop Construction Company, should probably be excluded from participation in the hearing. However, we see no conflict with respect to Ms. Jones.  Acknowledging that she rents an apartment at Watergate, as far as we know, she is being offered exactly the same terms as offered to the other 2,000 other renters at Watergate.  We see no conflict, and we disagree with your opinion, dated January 31, 1978 or July 31, 1978, that she should not be able to vote on this matter.  We think there is no reason that she should be excluded from voting.  We would like to see you amend your opinion in this regard.

With respect to our request above to receive timely notice of developments, we hope that in good faith you will agree to this.  A few weeks ago, as you have acknowledged to me publicly and privately, you withheld two public documents from the Tenants Association upon advisement of someone still unnamed - I hope that you will be able to be more cooperative now.

Sincerely yours,

CB:rms                                    Chris Burns
                                          President
cc:  Mr. Daniel Lowenstein, Chairman      Watergate Tenants Association
     Fair Political Practices Commission

     Emeryville Planning Commission:              Emeryville City Council:
     Sally Benedetti    Edna Stevens             Wallace Fox, Mayor
     George Curran      Patricia Jones           Wylie Eaton, Vice-Mayor
     Thomas Enos        Michael Flynn            Richard Kassis
     Gary Kalian                                 Rena Rickles
                                                 Esther Spriggs
     Mr. Mark Buell

Committees: Security □ Maintenance □ Education and Bylaws □ Recreational and Social Activities

WATERGATE TENANTS ASSOCIATION
6-A ADMIRAL DRIVE, #481
Emeryville, California 94608

August 17, 1978


Mr. Ed Margason
Woodward-Clyde Consultants
Civil Engineers
P. O. Box 24075
Oakland, California  94623


Dear Mr. Margason:

I'm sure that you are probably aware of the planned conversion of Watergate
Apartments to condominiums. At the August 16th "work session" of the Emery-
ville City Planning Commission, Jose Hurtado, City Engineer, indicated that
a predecessor firm to yours rendered an opinion on the soil conditions prior
to initial approval of the Watergate construction by City Council.  At
yesterday's meeting, Mr. Hurtado indicated that, his opinion was that your
initial report, occurring around 1969 or earlier, was still valid in 1978,
and that there would be no need for an update at this time.

We have three questions to you at this time:

     1.  Can we have a copy of your findings in 1969 or earlier?
         We will be glad to pay for any copying charges involved.

     2.  Would your firm, in 1978, be willing to state that whatever
         conclusions you arrived at in 1969, or before, are unchanged
         now, based upon conditions existing now or would you need to
         make another study now, nine years later? It has been implied
         that no further studies are necessary now.

     3.  If your answer is that no further studies are needed now,
         would you be willing to render an opinion to Emeryville
         City Council to that effect, with a copy to this Association?


                    . . .         Continued    . . . . .


Committees: Security □ Maintenance □ Education and Bylaws □ Recreational and Social Activities

Mr. Ed Margason
Woodward-Clyde Consultants
August 17, 1978
Page 2


We would appreciate a very timely reply to these questions. If you have
any questions of me, the phone number of my client on any given day (I am
self-employed), can be obtained by calling (415) 653-7378. On Friday,
August 18th, I can be reached at (415) 641-6675.

Thank you.

Sincerely yours,

[illegible]

Chris Burns
President
Watergate Tenants Association

On June 1, 1978 we became the new owners of the Watergate Apartments.
Our group is composed of the Pacific Union Company of San Francisco
and the Watergate Company. We are proud of the Watergate apartment
complex and look forward to the challenge of maintaining and improving
the pleasant quality of life in this community.

Our first objective is to manage Watergate as the best apartment complex
possible.

We plan to go slowly and make as few changes as possible until we are

3701 Buchanan Street
San Francisco

Phone (415) 929-7100

July 13, 1978

Dear Watergate Resident:

We would like to give you specific information regarding our conversion plans
here at Watergate.

It is our hope that this complex will continue to provide you with a unique home
lifestyle, close to the geographical center of the San Francisco Bay Area. Our
plan is to provide each of you the increased advantage of having the opportunity
to own the unit in which you reside. This will be done by converting the apartment
complex from one ownership to condominiums, where 1,249 individuals own their own
unit and share the ownership of the common areas such as land, streets, Clipper
Club, etc., through a homeowner's association.

While we are conducting this process, the Company has agreed for the present not
to increase the rents to current residents and to offer six month renewals on
expired leases.

We plan to keep the present operational staff and only make administrative changes
where it is deemed an improvement.

There are tremendous advantages to you in the conversion process and we hope those
of you who plan on staying here recognize the benefits. In addition to being in
a position to purchase your home at a favorable price, several improvements will
occur here at Watergate.

Watergate Company intends as part of its plans, to correct the deficiencies that
have been identified in the project, with special emphasis on security, water
leaks and common area rehabilitation. It has been shown that people will have
a greater commitment because of their decision to own here and the population will
become less transient. The City of Emeryville will share in the benefits of a
stable population.

We are aware that there are those of you who are not in a financial position to
consider buying or who will elect not to buy but would like to stay on as renters.
In this regard, you should understand that we expect the phasing program to be

[illegible]

C

Watergate Residents                                                    Page Two
                                                                                 July 13, 1978

such that the units will be sold over a three to four year period. During this
time, the unsold units will continue to be available as rentals. In addition,
even after all the units have been sold, there will be a significant number of
units available as rentals since some buyers will choose not to live in the pro-
ject.

In order for us to get this project into a position that it can be sold to you,
a number of things must occur. The entire project must be engineered for load
subdivision spaces. The City Planning Department must approve the process and the
City Council must confirm the Planning Department's approval. Also, protective
covenants and restrictions and by-laws must be approved by the California Division
of Real Estate. Lenders must commit to provide loans for the units and most im-
portantly, "the marketplace" must approve in order for sales to be made."

You are in the attractive position of having all of this take place before you
make a decision about your purchase. We would like to present several of the
positive reasons why you should consider purchasing your unit.

PRICE:     Although we cannot quote an exact price for your unit until we have
           received all of the necessary approvals, we have received permission
           from the Division of Real Estate to quote price ranges. Please note
           that these proposed market price ranges are effective as of July,
           1978, i.e., as if the project could be sold today.

                                                           Average Price
                    Type              Square Footage      Prices From    Less Than

                    Studio            470 - 530 sq. ft.   $29,900        $35,000
                    Jr. 1 Bedroom     530 - 716 sq. ft.   $35,000        $39,900
                    1 Bedroom         668 - 944 sq. ft.   $43,000        $55,000
                    2 Bedroom         890 -1250 sq. ft.   $65,000        $80,000

           The fact that there are 92 different floor plans, all with different
views and various interior conditions, must be considered in determining
the final price. The prices for units with extra desirability
because of view or location will be higher than the price averages
quoted above.

           These price ranges are significantly under market value. While our
experience indicates that these prices will increase to the general
public, the units will be offered within these price ranges to Water-
gate residents. Watergate residents will receive a 6% discount from the actual
prices established in the range indicated. A decorator's allowance
will be offered for each unit as follows: Studio, $1,000; Junior
One-Bedroom, $1,250; One-Bedroom, $1,500; and Two-Bedroom, $2,500.

sections.question: The initial question is whether or not an ordinance requiring approval of 50% plus 1 of the existing tenants before approval of the City Council and the Planning Commission should be enacted. We believe that the members of City Council should be allowed to vote on this matter affecting more than 50% of the adults in this City.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether three Emeryville City Council members who reside at Watergate Apartments face a conflict of interest in voting on condominium conversion approval, given that residents receive a 6% discount on unit purchases, and whether these Council members should be permitted to vote on the matter despite constituting a majority of the Council.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a tenant association to the FPPC, not an FPPC response. The document includes supporting materials (letters from property management, tenant response letters) but no FPPC conclusion or advice. The question section is synthesized from the body of the July 23rd letter. OCR quality is moderate with some scanning artifacts.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The initial question is whether or not an ordinance requiring approval of 50% plus 1 of the existing tenants before approval of the City Council and the Planning Commission should be enacted. We believe that the members of City Council should be allowed to vote on this matter affecting more than 50% of the adults in this City.

CONCLUSION: s you arrived at in 1969, or before, are unchanged 
now, based upon conditions existing now or would you need to 
make another study now, nine years later? It has been implied 
that no further studies are necessary now. 
3. 
If your answer is that no further studies are needed now, 
would you be willing to render an opinion to Emeryville 
City Council to that effect, with a copy to this Association? 
Continued

embedding.first_500_words: WATERGATE TENANTS ASSOCIATION 9 COMMODORE DRIVE, #304 Emeryville, California 94608 July 24, 1978 Fair Political Practices Commission 1100 "K" Street P. O. Box 807 Sacramento, California 95804 Attn: Mr. Daniel H. Lowenstein, Chairman RE: Conflict of Interest Laws Applicable to Emeryville City Council Members Dear Sirs: This letter is a confirmation of our July 23rd Mailgram to you about the above matter. In this regard, we enclose copies of the following: 1. Our July 23rd letter sent to you as a Mailgram. 2. The June 15th letter from Pacific Union Company, representing their initial communication to the tenants and proposing a month-to-month tenancy when leases expire. 3. Our June 29th letter to Pacific Union Company. 4. Their July 13th letter to tenants outlining some tentative prices, etc. 5. Our July 21st letter to the Chairman of the Planning Commission calling for consideration of our proposed ordinance before final approval of the proposal is granted. We would like your timely consideration of the questions posed in our July 23rd letter. Sincerely yours, Michael Levin President ML:rms Encls. cc: Mr. Chris Burns Acting Vice-President Watergate Tenants Association Emeryville City Council Members (w/enclosures) Wallace Fox - Mayor Wylie Eaton - Vice-Mayor Richard Kassis Rena Rickles Esther Spriggs Committees: Security □ Maintenance □ Education and Bylaws □ Recreational and Social Activities WATERGATE TENANTS ASSOCIATION 9 COMMODORE DRIVE, #304 Emeryville, California 94608 July 23, 1978 Fair Political Practices Commission 1100 "K" Street P.O. Box 807 Sacramento, California 95804 Attn: Mr. Daniel H. Lowenstein, Chairman RE: Conflict Of Interest Laws Applicable To Emeryville City Council Members Dear Sirs: Thank you for sending us a copy of the July 14th letter to you from James White, City Attorney for Emeryville. The purpose of this letter is to present additional background information to you which we believe to be relevant in considering Mr. White's request, and to present to you our point of view. It should be understood that this proposed conversion is not a "routine" conversion. In size, it is the largest conversion ever attempted in California and, as far as we know, it is the largest such attempt ever in the United States. Watergate represents a unique situation in America. With 2000 residents, we represent more than 50% of the adult population of Emeryville. Of 2,608 registered voters in Emeryville, 53% or 1,267 live at Watergate. The 2,000 residents have opted for a life in an alternative complex having no children and, if the con- version is completed, there will be no comparable housing available nearby for those who cannot afford what appears to be very high prices for units constructed subject to minimum building codes on filled land. So much for background. With respect to the conflict of interest described in Mr. White's letter, it is based on the fact that the three City Council Members who live at Watergate will be "permitted to purchase their unit for approximately $4,000 less than the purchase price offered to the general public." In actuality, the discount will
embedding.summary: The Watergate Tenants Association requests FPPC guidance on whether three Emeryville City Council members residing at Watergate Apartments have a conflict of interest in voting on a condominium conversion proposal that would offer residents a 6% discount on purchases. The Association argues no conflict exists since all residents receive the same discount and seeks confirmation that Council members should be permitted to vote on the matter.
